intro: Centrostachys fasciculata, commonly known as the "false pennyroyal," is a plant species in the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and fever reduction, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Centrostachys fasciculata include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Centrostachys fasciculata is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared for the treatment of colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
